# CardConjurer Synthetic Renderer Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Replace [synthetic.py](src/moxify_ocr/data/synthetic.py) (the Montserrat-based PIL renderer) with a pre-rendered pool of bottom-region crops generated by driving CardConjurer via Playwright. Real Beleren / Gotham fonts, full control over set / language / rarity / collector-number / foil, and the same `(image, label)` shape `dataset.py` already consumes.

**Architecture:** CardConjurer runs in a docker container on `localhost:4242` (bind-mount static files into nginx — no 2.5 GB image build). A one-shot Python script drives a headless Chromium via Playwright, applies a card spec to the page, captures `window.bottomInfoCanvas` (the offscreen text-only layer that holds the bottom strip), composites on black, and writes a `(48, 256, 3)` PNG plus a JSONL label entry. Pre-rendered ~50K cards live under `data/synth_cardconjurer/`. The training dataset adds a new branch parallel to the line-compositor; old `synthetic.py` is removed once the new path is wired in.

**Tech stack:** Python 3.11 + uv, Playwright (sync API), Pillow, TensorFlow / `tf.data` (existing). Docker (existing host install) for the CardConjurer service.

**Scope (this plan):** bottom-region only. The exact same renderer can later (Phase 2 — out of scope here) load a frame template + DFC transform layers to render full-card images for the planned card-name OCR. The script and spec generator are designed for that extension.

**Spike artifacts already produced:**
- [scripts/spike_cardconjurer.py](scripts/spike_cardconjurer.py) — proof of concept.
- [artifacts/cardconjurer_spike/contact_sheet.png](artifacts/cardconjurer_spike/contact_sheet.png) — 10 variety samples.
- Throughput observed: ~1.1s per card. 50K cards → ~15 hours one-shot pre-render.

---

## Background reading for the implementer

Before starting, read in this order:

1. [src/moxify_ocr/data/labels.py](src/moxify_ocr/data/labels.py) — the canonical label format. `make_label({...}, is_foil=...)` returns `"<num>/<total> <rarity>\n<set> <pip> <lang>"` where pip is `★` (foil) or `•` (non-foil). Our renderer's output must match this exactly.
2. [src/moxify_ocr/data/synthetic.py](src/moxify_ocr/data/synthetic.py) — what we're replacing. Note `generate_synthetic_crop(seed) → (np.ndarray (48,256,3), label_str)`.
3. [src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py:97-228](src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py) — the integration point. The `synthetic_ratio` knob and the inline branch around line 209 are what we're swapping out.
4. [src/moxify_ocr/data/line_compositor.py](src/moxify_ocr/data/line_compositor.py) — the *other* synthetic leg (real-data half-stitching). Mirror its style for the new module.
5. [scripts/spike_cardconjurer.py](scripts/spike_cardconjurer.py) — the working renderer logic. Tasks 3 and 4 reuse its core.
6. [docs/plans/2026-04-23-bottom-region-extractor-plan.md](docs/plans/2026-04-23-bottom-region-extractor-plan.md) — example of the plan style this repo uses (more conversational than rigid TDD steps; we keep TDD but allow the prose).

## Mapping CardConjurer templates to our label format

**topLeft template** (CardConjurer M15 default): `{rarity} {number}`. **Our label format**: `{number} {rarity}`. → patch `card.bottomInfo.topLeft.text` to swap order.

**midLeft template** (CardConjurer M15 default): `{set} • {language}  {savex}{fontbelerenbsc}…￮{savex2}{artist}` — i.e., set/lang separator AND foil-pip near artist. **Our label format**: `{set} {pip} {language}` only — no artist, single pip controlled by foil bool. → patch `card.bottomInfo.midLeft.text` to drop the artist-side suffix and inject `★` or `•` between set and lang.

**Foil** is purely a string substitution at render time. The `{fontbelerenbsc}*` glyph route (used by `toggleStarDot`) is *not* what we want — we want the literal `★` (U+2605) and `•` (U+2022) glyphs in the same Gotham Medium that renders the rest of the line, matching what real MTG cards show after the camera captures them.

(Two synthetic add-ons were prototyped during the spike and dropped: the per-color M15 holo-stamp seal and the "The List" planeswalker shield. Both were programmatically compositable from CardConjurer's static assets but visibly intersected the set-code/collector-number text in our crop — confusing the OCR target rather than helping it. Real cards show these on the bottom strip too, but augmentation-time noise + the existing real-data line-compositor already cover that variance. If we ever want either back, the assets are `/data/images/cardImages/m15/holoStamps/m15HoloStamp{W|U|B|R|G|M|A|A2|A3|L}.png` and `/data/images/cardImages/stamp.png` respectively.)

---

## Task 1: Vendor a starter container script

**Why:** the spike used an ad-hoc `docker run` against an unrelated `moxify-ml` image. Productionize this so anyone can bring CardConjurer up reproducibly.

**Files:**
- Create: `infra/cardconjurer/README.md`
- Create: `infra/cardconjurer/start.sh`
- Create: `infra/cardconjurer/stop.sh`

**Step 1: Write `infra/cardconjurer/README.md`**

Document:
- One-time setup: download the CardConjurer tarball (`curl -L -o /tmp/cardconjurer.tar.gz https://github.com/Investigamer/cardconjurer/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz && tar -xzf /tmp/cardconjurer.tar.gz -C /tmp/`).
- That the container is bind-mounted, no docker build needed.
- That the renderer expects `localhost:4242/creator/` to respond.
- Note the **2.5 GB** repo size — caution against `git clone`-ing it directly.

**Step 2: Write `infra/cardconjurer/start.sh`**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SRC_DIR="${CARDCONJURER_SRC:-/tmp/cardconjurer-master}"
if [ ! -d "$SRC_DIR" ]; then
  echo "missing $SRC_DIR — see README" >&2
  exit 1
fi
docker rm -f cardconjurer 2>/dev/null || true
docker run -d --rm --name cardconjurer -p 4242:4242 \
  -v "$SRC_DIR:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro" \
  -v "$SRC_DIR/app.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro" \
  nginx:alpine
echo "waiting for cardconjurer ..."
until curl -sf -o /dev/null http://localhost:4242/; do sleep 1; done
echo "ready: http://localhost:4242/creator/"
```

**Step 3: Write `infra/cardconjurer/stop.sh`**

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
docker rm -f cardconjurer 2>/dev/null || true
```

**Step 4: Make them executable, smoke-test**

Run:
```bash
chmod +x infra/cardconjurer/start.sh infra/cardconjurer/stop.sh
infra/cardconjurer/start.sh
curl -sf -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://localhost:4242/creator/  # expect 200
infra/cardconjurer/stop.sh
```

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add infra/cardconjurer/
git commit -m "feat(infra): cardconjurer dev container scripts"
```

## Task 3: `cardconjurer_specs.py` — random spec generator (TDD)

**Why:** the renderer needs a stream of `CardSpec` objects with realistic random fields. Pure Python, easy to test in isolation.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_specs.py`
- Test: `tests/data/test_cardconjurer_specs.py`

**Step 1: Write the failing tests**

```python
# tests/data/test_cardconjurer_specs.py
"""Tests for the random card-spec generator."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

import pytest

from moxify_ocr.data.cardconjurer_specs import CardSpec, generate_specs, make_spec


def test_make_spec_is_deterministic_with_seed() -> None:
    a = make_spec(seed=42)
    b = make_spec(seed=42)
    assert a == b


def test_make_spec_produces_valid_label() -> None:
    spec = make_spec(seed=0)
    # Set is a 3-letter or 4-letter code from a known list.
    assert 2 <= len(spec.info_set) <= 5
    # Rarity is a single uppercase letter.
    assert spec.info_rarity in {"C", "U", "R", "M", "S", "P"}
    # Language is from our supported set.
    assert spec.info_language in {"EN", "DE", "FR", "ES", "IT", "PT", "JA", "KO", "ZH", "RU"}


def test_generate_specs_yields_n_specs() -> None:
    specs = list(generate_specs(n=100, seed=0))
    assert len(specs) == 100
    # Should be diverse — at least 3 different languages in 100 draws.
    langs = Counter(s.info_language for s in specs)
    assert len(langs) >= 3


def test_collector_number_formats_are_diverse() -> None:
    """Realistic distribution: most numeric, a few X-prefixed, some no /total."""
    specs = list(generate_specs(n=500, seed=0))
    has_slash = sum("/" in s.info_number for s in specs)
    has_x = sum(s.info_number.upper().startswith("X") for s in specs)
    # Majority should have /total (matches modern manifest distribution).
    assert has_slash > 250
    # X-prefix is rare but must exist (>0, <10% so not over-represented).
    assert 0 < has_x < 50


def test_foil_distribution_is_realistic() -> None:
    specs = list(generate_specs(n=1000, seed=0))
    foil_count = sum(s.foil for s in specs)
    # Real MTG distribution: foils are minority but not negligible.
    assert 100 < foil_count < 400
```

**Step 2: Run tests, verify they fail**

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/data/test_cardconjurer_specs.py -v
```

Expected: FAIL with `ModuleNotFoundError: moxify_ocr.data.cardconjurer_specs`.

**Step 3: Implement the module**

```python
# src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_specs.py
"""Random card-spec generator for the CardConjurer renderer.

Produces a stream of :class:`CardSpec` objects with realistic distributions
across set code, language, rarity, collector-number format, and foil
treatment. Each call seeded for determinism so the generated training pool
is reproducible.

The spec captures only what the renderer drives — not what the OCR labels
read. The corresponding label is derived deterministically from the spec by
the renderer (see ``scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py``).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import random
import string
from collections.abc import Iterator
from dataclasses import dataclass

# Realistic distributions tuned to match what the manifest already contains.
_LANG_WEIGHTS = {
    "EN": 0.45, "DE": 0.10, "FR": 0.10, "ES": 0.08, "IT": 0.07,
    "PT": 0.05, "JA": 0.07, "KO": 0.04, "ZH": 0.03, "RU": 0.01,
}
_RARITY_WEIGHTS = {"C": 0.50, "U": 0.30, "R": 0.15, "M": 0.04, "S": 0.005, "P": 0.005}
# A small pool of plausible 3-4 letter set codes — enough variety without
# pretending to be exhaustive (the OCR doesn't need to know real set semantics).
_SET_CODES = (
    "LEA LEB 2ED 3ED 4ED 5ED 6ED 7ED 8ED 9ED 10E "
    "MIR VIS WTH TMP STH EXO USG ULG UDS MMQ NEM PCY "
    "ICE ALL CSP TSP PLC FUT 10E LRW MOR SHM EVE ALA "
    "CON ARB ZEN WWK ROE M11 SOM MBS NPH CMD M12 ISD "
    "DKA AVR M13 RTR GTC DGM M14 THS BNG JOU M15 KTK "
    "FRF DTK ORI BFZ OGW SOI EMN KLD AER AKH HOU XLN "
    "RIX DOM M19 GRN RNA WAR MH1 M20 ELD THB IKO M21 "
    "ZNR KHM STX MH2 AFR MID VOW NEO SNC HBG DMU BRO "
    "ONE MOM MAT WOE LCI MKM OTJ BIG MH3"
).split()


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CardSpec:
    """One card to render.

    ``foil`` controls whether the pip between set and lang is ★ or •. All
    other fields are passed verbatim to CardConjurer's #info-* DOM inputs.
    """

    info_set: str
    info_language: str
    info_rarity: str
    info_number: str
    foil: bool


def _weighted_choice(rng: random.Random, weights: dict[str, float]) -> str:
    return rng.choices(list(weights.keys()), weights=list(weights.values()), k=1)[0]


def _gen_collector_number(rng: random.Random) -> str:
    """Most numeric NNN/NNN, some bare NNN, a few X-prefixed, occasional pre-modern."""
    r = rng.random()
    if r < 0.05:
        # X-prefixed (promo): "X12", "X007"
        return f"X{rng.randint(1, 999):0{rng.choice([2, 3])}d}"
    if r < 0.15:
        # Bare collector number: "042"
        return f"{rng.randint(1, 999):03d}"
    # Numeric NNN/NNN — by far the common case.
    total = rng.choice([100, 150, 200, 250, 277, 280, 302, 350])
    num = rng.randint(1, total)
    return f"{num:03d}/{total:03d}"


def make_spec(seed: int) -> CardSpec:
    """Build one deterministic CardSpec from a seed."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    return CardSpec(
        info_set=rng.choice(_SET_CODES),
        info_language=_weighted_choice(rng, _LANG_WEIGHTS),
        info_rarity=_weighted_choice(rng, _RARITY_WEIGHTS),
        info_number=_gen_collector_number(rng),
        foil=rng.random() < 0.20,  # ~20% foil — realistic for booster mix
    )


def generate_specs(n: int, seed: int = 0) -> Iterator[CardSpec]:
    """Yield ``n`` specs derived deterministically from a base seed."""
    for i in range(n):
        yield make_spec(seed=seed * 1_000_003 + i)
```

**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass**

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/data/test_cardconjurer_specs.py -v
```

Expected: 5 passes.

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_specs.py tests/data/test_cardconjurer_specs.py
git commit -m "feat(data): random card-spec generator for cardconjurer renderer"
```

## Task 4: `render_cardconjurer_pool.py` — the actual renderer

**Why:** consumes specs from Task 3, drives CardConjurer via Playwright, writes PNG + JSONL output.

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py`

This is a one-shot script, not a library — no unit tests. Smoke-test it in Task 5 instead.

**Step 1: Implement the renderer**

Pull the working core from `scripts/spike_cardconjurer.py` and adapt:

- Take `--n`, `--seed`, `--out-dir`, `--cardconjurer-url` CLI args
- Stream specs from `generate_specs(n, seed)`
- For each spec:
  - Patch `card.bottomInfo.topLeft.text = '{elemidinfo-number} {kerning3}{elemidinfo-rarity}{kerning0}'` (label-format match)
  - Patch `card.bottomInfo.midLeft.text = '{elemidinfo-set} ★ {elemidinfo-language}'` if foil else `'… • …'`
  - Set the six `#info-*` fields
  - `await bottomInfoEdited()`
  - Capture `window.bottomInfoCanvas`, composite on black
  - Crop to bottom strip
  - Resize to `(256, 48)` to match production crop
  - Save to `{out_dir}/images/{seed:08d}.png`
  - Append a JSONL row with `{image_path, label, foil, info_set, ...}` to `{out_dir}/labels.jsonl`
- Restart the Chromium tab every 1000 cards (defensive against memory leaks; Playwright contexts can leak). Same browser, fresh context.
- Print progress every 100 cards with elapsed time + ETA.

**The label** is constructed in Python (NOT read from the rendered image), built from the spec to match `make_label`'s output:
```python
pip = "★" if spec.foil else "•"
label = f"{spec.info_number} {spec.info_rarity}\n{spec.info_set} {pip} {spec.info_language}"
```

**Step 2: One-shot dry run with --n 5**

```bash
infra/cardconjurer/start.sh
.venv/bin/python scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py --n 5 --seed 0 \
  --out-dir /tmp/cc_test_pool
ls /tmp/cc_test_pool/images/ | head
cat /tmp/cc_test_pool/labels.jsonl | head -3
```

Expected: 5 PNG files (256×48 each), 5 JSONL rows.

**Step 3: Verify rendered images aren't blank**

```bash
.venv/bin/python -c "
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np
for i in range(5):
    img = Image.open(f'/tmp/cc_test_pool/images/{i:08d}.png').convert('RGB')
    arr = np.asarray(img)
    nonblack = (arr.sum(axis=-1) > 30).sum()
    print(i, 'nonblack pixels:', nonblack)
    assert nonblack > 100, 'rendered image looks empty'
"
```

Expected: each row reports >100 nonblack pixels.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py
git commit -m "feat(scripts): cardconjurer renderer driving playwright"
```

If user says no — diagnose, fix, re-render, re-verify. Don't proceed to Task 6 until validated.

## Task 6: `cardconjurer_dataset.py` — read pre-rendered pool (TDD)

**Why:** `dataset.py` needs a function that yields `(image, label_str)` from the pre-rendered pool, parallel to `generate_synthetic_crop`.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_dataset.py`
- Test: `tests/data/test_cardconjurer_dataset.py`

**Step 1: Write failing tests**

```python
# tests/data/test_cardconjurer_dataset.py
"""Tests for the pre-rendered CardConjurer pool reader."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

from moxify_ocr.data.cardconjurer_dataset import CardConjurerPool, sample_from_pool


def _write_fake_pool(root: Path, n: int = 5) -> None:
    """Write n fake (256x48 black) PNGs + labels.jsonl matching the renderer output."""
    (root / "images").mkdir(parents=True)
    rows = []
    for i in range(n):
        img_path = f"images/{i:08d}.png"
        Image.new("RGB", (256, 48), (i * 10, 0, 0)).save(root / img_path)
        rows.append({"image_path": img_path, "label": f"00{i}/100 R\nMID • EN"})
    with (root / "labels.jsonl").open("w") as f:
        for r in rows:
            f.write(json.dumps(r) + "\n")


def test_load_pool_finds_all_entries(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    _write_fake_pool(tmp_path, n=5)
    pool = CardConjurerPool.load(tmp_path)
    assert len(pool) == 5


def test_sample_returns_correct_shape_and_label(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    _write_fake_pool(tmp_path, n=5)
    pool = CardConjurerPool.load(tmp_path)
    img, label = sample_from_pool(pool, seed=0)
    assert img.shape == (48, 256, 3)
    assert img.dtype == np.uint8
    assert "\n" in label


def test_sample_is_deterministic(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    _write_fake_pool(tmp_path, n=10)
    pool = CardConjurerPool.load(tmp_path)
    a = sample_from_pool(pool, seed=42)
    b = sample_from_pool(pool, seed=42)
    assert np.array_equal(a[0], b[0])
    assert a[1] == b[1]


def test_empty_pool_raises(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    (tmp_path / "labels.jsonl").touch()
    pool = CardConjurerPool.load(tmp_path)
    import pytest

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        sample_from_pool(pool, seed=0)
```

**Step 2: Run tests, verify failure**

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/data/test_cardconjurer_dataset.py -v
```

Expected: FAIL — `ModuleNotFoundError`.

**Step 3: Implement the module**

```python
# src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_dataset.py
"""Pre-rendered CardConjurer pool reader for the dataset pipeline."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import random
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
from PIL import Image


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class _PoolEntry:
    image_path: Path  # absolute
    label: str


@dataclass
class CardConjurerPool:
    """In-memory index of (image_path, label) pairs from a pre-rendered pool."""

    entries: list[_PoolEntry]

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, root: Path) -> "CardConjurerPool":
        """Read ``{root}/labels.jsonl`` and resolve relative image paths.

        ``root`` is the directory the renderer wrote to — contains
        ``labels.jsonl`` and an ``images/`` subdir.
        """
        manifest = root / "labels.jsonl"
        if not manifest.exists():
            return cls(entries=[])
        entries: list[_PoolEntry] = []
        with manifest.open() as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                row = json.loads(line)
                entries.append(
                    _PoolEntry(
                        image_path=root / row["image_path"],
                        label=row["label"],
                    )
                )
        return cls(entries=entries)

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        return len(self.entries)


def sample_from_pool(
    pool: CardConjurerPool, *, seed: int
) -> tuple[np.ndarray, str]:
    """Draw one (image, label) pair deterministically from the pool."""
    if not pool.entries:
        raise ValueError("CardConjurerPool is empty — nothing to sample from")
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    entry = rng.choice(pool.entries)
    img = np.asarray(Image.open(entry.image_path).convert("RGB"), dtype=np.uint8)
    return img, entry.label
```

**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass**

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/data/test_cardconjurer_dataset.py -v
```

Expected: 4 passes.

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/moxify_ocr/data/cardconjurer_dataset.py tests/data/test_cardconjurer_dataset.py
git commit -m "feat(data): cardconjurer pool reader for dataset pipeline"
```

## Task 7: Wire the new leg into `dataset.py`

**Why:** the existing `synthetic_ratio` knob and inline branch (around [dataset.py:97-228](src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py)) need to point at the new pool reader.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py`
- Modify: `tests/data/test_dataset.py` (add a coverage test)

**Step 1: Read how the existing path is wired**

```bash
grep -n "synthetic_ratio\|generate_synthetic_crop\|synth_ids" src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py
```

Note exact line ranges to modify.

**Step 2: Add config field**

In `DatasetConfig` (around `dataset.py:97`), add alongside `synthetic_ratio`:

```python
# Path to a pre-rendered CardConjurer pool. When set + ratio > 0, samples
# are drawn from this pool instead of the deprecated synthetic.py renderer.
cardconjurer_pool: Path | None = None
cardconjurer_ratio: float = 0.0
```

**Step 3: Replace the inline branch**

Around `dataset.py:199-228`, replace the `from moxify_ocr.data.synthetic import generate_synthetic_crop` branch with:

```python
from moxify_ocr.data.cardconjurer_dataset import (
    CardConjurerPool,
    sample_from_pool,
)

cc_pool: CardConjurerPool | None = None
if config.cardconjurer_ratio > 0 and config.cardconjurer_pool is not None:
    cc_pool = CardConjurerPool.load(config.cardconjurer_pool)
    if not cc_pool.entries:
        raise ValueError(
            f"cardconjurer_pool={config.cardconjurer_pool} is empty — "
            "did you run scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py?"
        )
```

And in the per-sample loop, replace the `if synthetic_ratio > 0 and rng.random() < synthetic_ratio:` block with:

```python
if cc_pool is not None and rng.random() < config.cardconjurer_ratio:
    cc_seed = seed * 1_000_003 + 10_000_000 + cc_counter
    cc_counter += 1
    cc_img, cc_label = sample_from_pool(cc_pool, seed=cc_seed)
    cc_ids = np.asarray(encode_label(cc_label), dtype=np.int32)
    yield cc_img, cc_ids, np.int32(len(cc_ids))
    continue
```

(The cc image is already (48, 256, 3) — no letterbox needed.)

**Step 4: Add a coverage test**

In `tests/data/test_dataset.py`, add a test that builds a tiny pool + manifest, asserts that with `cardconjurer_ratio=1.0` the dataset only yields cc samples.

**Step 5: Run all data tests**

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/data/ -v
```

Expected: all green.

**Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py tests/data/test_dataset.py
git commit -m "feat(data): wire cardconjurer pool into dataset config"
```

---

## Task 8: Pre-render the 50K pool

**Why:** one-shot. Produces the actual training data.

**Files:**
- Modify: `data/synth_cardconjurer/` (gitignored output)
- Modify: `.gitignore`

**Step 1: Add gitignore entry**

```bash
echo 'data/synth_cardconjurer/' >> .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git commit -m "chore: gitignore cardconjurer rendered pool"
```

**Step 2: Run the renderer**

```bash
infra/cardconjurer/start.sh
.venv/bin/python scripts/render_cardconjurer_pool.py --n 50000 --seed 0 \
  --out-dir data/synth_cardconjurer/v1
```

Expected: ~15 hours. Run overnight. Output: 50K PNGs + 1 labels.jsonl (~10 GB).

**Step 3: Verify pool integrity**

```bash
.venv/bin/python -c "
from pathlib import Path
from moxify_ocr.data.cardconjurer_dataset import CardConjurerPool
pool = CardConjurerPool.load(Path('data/synth_cardconjurer/v1'))
print(f'pool size: {len(pool)}')
assert len(pool) >= 49500, 'too many failed renders'
"
```

Expected: pool size near 50K (allow <1% failures).

**Step 4: Stop the container**

```bash
infra/cardconjurer/stop.sh
```

---

## Task 9: Train v3 with the new leg, confirm CER doesn't regress

**Why:** the whole point. v2 plateaued at val_cer ~0.43; if the new synthetic data helps, we expect lower CER.

**Files:**
- Modify: `configs/v2.yml` → copy to `configs/v3.yml` (or whatever the existing config naming is — check first)
- Modify: training entrypoint as needed

**Step 1: Check the current training config layout**

```bash
ls configs/
cat configs/v2.yml 2>/dev/null || cat configs/$(ls configs/ | tail -1)
```

**Step 2: Create `configs/v3.yml`** with `cardconjurer_pool: data/synth_cardconjurer/v1` and `cardconjurer_ratio: 0.30`. Set `synthetic_ratio: 0.0` (deprecate the old leg).

**Step 3: Run training**

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m moxify_ocr.train --config configs/v3.yml --epochs 10
```

Compare val_cer at epoch 10 against v2 baseline. Expected: lower or at least equal.

**Step 4: Commit config + log a note in the training doc**

```bash
git add configs/v3.yml
# Append a paragraph to ocr_training_doc.md describing v3 results
git add ocr_training_doc.md
git commit -m "feat(train): v3 config with cardconjurer synthetic pool"
```

---

## Task 10: Deprecate `synthetic.py`

**Why:** new path validated. Old leg adds noise and a fonts-licensing footnote (Montserrat is fine but let's not maintain two synthetic legs).

**Files:**
- Delete: `src/moxify_ocr/data/synthetic.py`
- Delete: `tests/data/test_synthetic.py`
- Modify: `src/moxify_ocr/data/dataset.py` — remove the `synthetic_ratio` field if v3 + ratio=0 is confirmed working
- Delete: `assets/Montserrat-Medium.ttf` (committed in 1fc917d)

**Step 1: Verify no other references**

```bash
grep -rn "from moxify_ocr.data.synthetic\|generate_synthetic_crop\|synthetic_ratio" --include='*.py' .
```

Should only show `dataset.py` (which we're modifying) and the test file (which we're deleting). Anything else needs to be cleaned up first.

**Step 2: Remove the files**

```bash
git rm src/moxify_ocr/data/synthetic.py tests/data/test_synthetic.py assets/Montserrat-Medium.ttf
```

**Step 3: Drop `synthetic_ratio` from `dataset.py`**

Remove the field from `DatasetConfig` and any remaining `synth_*` variables. Run the test suite:

```bash
.venv/bin/pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all green.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "refactor(data): remove deprecated synthetic.py renderer"
```

---

## Acceptance criteria

The plan is complete when:

1. ✅ All tests in `tests/data/` pass.
2. ✅ `infra/cardconjurer/start.sh` brings up a working CardConjurer locally in <30s.
3. ✅ `data/synth_cardconjurer/v1/` contains ≥49,500 valid PNGs.
4. ✅ A v3 training run hits val_cer ≤ v2's val_cer at the same epoch count.
5. ✅ `synthetic.py` and Montserrat-Medium.ttf are gone from the tree.

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **Full-card renders with frames.** We stop at `bottomInfoCanvas` capture. Frame loading is non-trivial (driving the autocomplete + frame-picker UI) and unnecessary for bottom-region OCR.
- **DFC / split-card renders.** These require a frame to be loaded; deferred to the planned card-name OCR work.
- **Online injection during training.** We pre-render to disk. Online injection would mean a Playwright dependency at training time, which is fragile.
- **Set-symbol images.** CardConjurer can fetch real set symbols from Scryfall on demand; we leave the symbol blank for the bottom-region renderer since OCR doesn't read the symbol image.

## Risks called out up-front

1. **The 50K render is 15 hours.** If we discover a label/glyph bug after it runs, we eat that time again. Task 5's smoke checkpoint exists specifically to de-risk this.
2. **`/creator/` is an htmx fragment without `<head>`.** Direct navigation does not load `css/style-9.css`, so the @font-face rules never register and canvas text renders in the serif fallback (looks Times-New-Roman-ish — not Gotham/Beleren). Fix in Task 4: after `page.goto`, inject `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/reset.css">` and `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/style-9.css">` into `document.head`, then call `document.fonts.load("24px gothammedium")` and friends explicitly (CSS @font-face is lazy — `document.fonts.ready` resolves immediately if no element has triggered a load yet).
3. **CardConjurer's init-order quirk.** A fresh page session has `enableCollectorInfo` checkbox unchecked despite localStorage being set to true, and `card.bottomInfoColor` is undefined. The renderer must explicitly check the box and set `card.bottomInfoColor = 'white'` before applying the first spec. Captured in Task 4 implementation.
3. **Browser memory leaks over a long run.** Mitigation: restart the page (not the browser) every 1000 cards. Also captured in Task 4.
4. **Image-reading hang.** Per memory: `Read` tool stalls on images. Implementer must use PIL/numpy via Bash to inspect rendered output, never `Read` an image directly.
